request.question: What was the second album by the singer who was a client of Andros Rodriguez, and whose debut album was  "Santogold"?

title: Santigold discography
The Explorers Club (band)
EggHunt Records
Andros Rodriguez
Against All Will
Santigold
Noah Georgeson
The Shortwave Set
Sarah Perrotta
Richard Davies (musician)
sentences: Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.
White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.
All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.
"Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.
The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.
The Explorers Club are a Pop rock band originally from the coast of South Carolina.
They are a 5-man band whose debut album was released by Dead Oceans.
The music of their first album is heavily influenced by the vocal harmony styles and production of The Beach Boys.
They are also influenced by classic rock and roll arrangements as made popular by The Beatles, Chuck Berry, Phil Spector, The Zombies, The Monkees and The Byrds.
Their second album shows more influences of soft-pop artists of the early-1970s such as Burt Bacharach, Glen Campbell, etc.
The band relocated to Nashville, TN in 2014 and released their third studio album Together on June 24, 2016
EggHunt Records is an independent record label based in Richmond, Virginia.
The label was founded by Adam Henceroth and Gregory Gendron in spring of 2014.
EggHunt Records focuses on upcoming, independent artists and releases material on cassette, CD, and vinyl physical formats, as well as digital download and streaming online.
EggHunt Records maintains an active roster of artists who have toured with notable acts such as The War on Drugs and Houndmouth.
EggHunt Records alumni include Lucy Dacus, whose debut album, "No Burden", was released on EggHunt Records in 2016.
In 2017, EggHunt Records signed Eric Slick of Dr. Dog to release his solo debut album, "Palisades".
Andros Rodriguez is a multi-platinum, Grammy award-winning music producer, engineer and mixer.

His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Against All Will is a rock quartet from Los Angeles, California, whose debut album lineup was formed in Spring 2009 by Jimmy Allen (formerly of Puddle of Mudd), Jeff Current (formerly of Seven Story Drop), Steve "Boomstick" Wilson (formerly of Dead Kennedys and t.A.T.u.), and Cello Dias (formerly of Soulfly).
Songs "All About You" and "The Drug I Need" from their debut album entitled A Rhyme & Reason ranked in the national rock radio top 50 in 2010.
Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.
Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.
Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.
In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".
Noah Georgeson is a Grammy winning musician, producer, and solo recording artist.
Georgeson's debut album "Find Shelter" was released through Plain Recordings on November 28, 2006.
Born in San Anselmo, California, he moved with his family to Nevada City, California at the age of three.

Georgeson studied classical guitar and music composition, receiving his BA in composition from San Francisco State University in 2001, and, with a recommendation from Terry Riley, he attended Mills College, receiving his Master of Fine Arts in 2003.
While at Mills, Georgeson studied with Fred Frith, Pauline Oliveros, and Alvin Curran.
Georgeson first found popular success as a part of San Francisco band The Pleased, along with fellow member Joanna Newsom, whose debut album "The Milk-Eyed Mender" he produced.

As a musician, producer, and mixer, Georgeson has since worked with Devendra Banhart, The Strokes, Little Joy, Bert Jansch, Charlotte Gainsbourg, Robin Pecknold, Mason Jennings, Cedric Bixler, Adam Green, Os Mutantes, Adan Jodorowsky, Harper Simon, Flo Morrissey, Cate Le Bon, and Rodrigo Amarante.
The Shortwave Set are a British alternative pop band whose debut album, "The Debt Collection", was released in 2005 on Independiente Records.
The band's music combines sample-based music with more traditional songwriting and instrumentation to create a sound the group have described as "Victorian Funk".
The band's second album, "Replica Sun Machine", was released on 12 May 2008 by Wall of Sound.
Sarah Perrotta (born 1979, United States) is a Hudson Valley based singer, songwriter and pianist.
Perrotta was the front woman of the indie-rock band Outloud Dreamer whose debut album “Drink The Sky” was named best modern rock album of 2000 by WKZE Radio 98.1FM.
Perrotta’s second album “The Well” was self-released in 2008 and features Tony Levin on bass and Garth Hudson on accordion.
The album was mixed by Grammy Award winner Malcolm Burn.
Her most recent record is entitled tonight released under her surname.
Richard Davies is an Australian-American musician.
He first came to prominence in the early 1990s as leader of the Australian band The Moles.
Upon moving to the United States, Davies joined with Eric Matthews to form Cardinal, whose debut album, "Cardinal," was released by Flydaddy in 1994.
He has since released several solo albums including "There's Never Been a Crowd Like This" (1996), "Telegraph" (1998), and "Barbarians" (2000).
In 2009 Davies and Guided by Voices front man Robert Pollard, collaborating under the name Cosmos, released an album, "Jar of Jam / Ton of Bricks."
In 2012 Davies and Matthews reformed Cardinal and released a second album, "Hymns."

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What was the second album by the singer who was a client of Andros Rodriguez, and whose debut album was  "Santogold"?
hop1_docs: Santigold | Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.  Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.  Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.  In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".

Santogold (album) | Santogold is the debut studio album by American artist Santigold (real name Santi White, who performed under the moniker Santogold at the time of the album's release).  The album was released on April 29, 2008 in the United States and May 12 in the United Kingdom, on Downtown Records and Atlantic Records, respectively.

Santigold discography | Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.  White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.  All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.  "Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.  The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

Andros Rodriguez | Andros Rodriguez is a multi-platinum, Grammy award-winning music producer, engineer and mixer.  His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Cold Fact | Cold Fact is the debut album from American singer-songwriter Rodriguez.  It was released in the United States on the Sussex label in March 1970.  The album sold very poorly in the United States (Rodriguez was himself an unknown in the US), but managed to sell well in both South Africa and Australia, with Rodriguez touring Australia in 1979.

Red. White. Green. | Red.  White.  Green.  is the second studio album by American deathcore band Upon a Burning Body.  The album was released on March 26, 2012 through Sumerian Records.  The album was produced by Will Putney, who produced their debut album, "The World Is Ours".  All of the songs on the album are titled after films that Robert Rodriguez produced or directed, continuing a trend set from their debut album.  It is the only album with Jonathan Gonzalez on drums and the last album with Chris "CJ" Johnson on guitar.
My Versions | My Versions is the debut album of Swedish singer Robin Stjernberg who was formerly a member of the boyband What's Up!  with Eric Saade and had placed second in Idol 2011.
response.summary_1: Bridge entity: Santigold (Santi White). Evidence: Andros Rodriguez's client list "include[s] ... Santigold" (passage 4); the singer's debut album is "Santogold" ("Her debut album, 'Santogold'", passage 1). Passage 1 further states the singer's second album as "Her second album, 'Master of My Make-Believe'." Next hop / retrieval direction: verify or cite Santigold (Santi White) discography (or sources on "Santogold"/Santigold) to confirm the second-album title "Master of My Make-Believe" (use the quoted phrases as search anchors).
response.hop2_query: Santigold discography "Master of My Make-Believe" second album
hop2_docs: Master of My Make-Believe | Master of My Make-Believe is the second studio album by American recording artist Santigold.  It was released in the United Kingdom on April 24, 2012 and in the United States on May 1, 2012, by Downtown Records and Atlantic Records.  The cover art representing four incarnations of Santigold was designed by Jason Schmidt.  The record was produced with a wide range of different collaborators, songwriters and producers; including her long-time friends of Switch and Diplo, as well as David Andrew Sitek of TV on the Radio and Nick Zinner of the Yeah Yeah Yeahs.

Santigold | Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.  Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.  Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.  In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".

Disparate Youth | "Disparate Youth" is a song from American singer-songwriter Santigold.  The track was released in the United States on 14 February 2012 as the lead single from the artist's second studio album, "Master of My Make-Believe" (2012).

Santigold discography | Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.  White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.  All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.  "Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.  The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

Believe Acoustic | Believe Acoustic is the third remix album by Canadian recording artist Justin Bieber.  It was released on January 29, 2013 through Island Records as the follow-up to his second remix album "" (2011).  This also marks the second acoustic album following "My Worlds Acoustic" (2010).  The album features acoustic and live versions of songs from his third studio album "Believe" (2012), as well as three new recordings.  The album went to number one on the US Billboard 200 chart making this Bieber's fifth number-one album and seventh consecutive top 10 album in the United States.

Are You Ready (Bucks Fizz album) | Are You Ready is the second studio album by the British pop group Bucks Fizz.  It was released on 26 April 1982 and features the UK No. 1 singles "The Land of Make Believe" and "My Camera Never Lies" as well as the follow-up "Now Those Days Are Gone".  It was produced by Andy Hill and released on RCA records.  It remains the group's most successful album.

My Truth | My Truth is the second studio album by Swedish recording artist Robyn.  It was released on 17 May 1999 through BMG Sweden.  Robyn collaborated with producers including Lindström & Ekhé, Christian Falk, Billy Mann, Thomas Rusiak and Masters at Work during its recording.  A pop and R&B record, "My Truth" is an autobiographical album for which Robyn co-wrote all fourteen songs.  Certain lyrics reflect on the abortion the singer had in the fall of 1998, which sparked controversy when RCA Records began formulating a North American release.  When she refused to make alterations to the album per request by the label, the plans to release "My Truth" outside of Sweden were cancelled.
response.summary_2: The singer (Santi White, stage name Santigold, formerly Santogold) released her second studio album, Master of My Make-Believe; passages confirm "Master of My Make-Believe" is Santigold's second album (released in 2012).
